mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 0.36% indicates deep accommodation
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payroll employment above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite strong payroll growth, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that portends earnings downgrades and equity multiple compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month by > 2%
evidence_refs: fact:current RSAFS at 446,887 million, HOUST at 1,178 thousand
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite dual demand contraction, or interaction effect (joint decline) shows no incremental predictive power beyond single-series models (F-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production below 102.0 (index 2017=100) signals supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 102.0 and remains there for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns when INDPRO < 102.0 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate rise exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a 3-month window signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite unemployment spike, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 3.0%) signals monetary tightening that constrains real purchasing power and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation pressure
failure_condition: Joint condition (inflation + high rates) shows no incremental predictive power beyond single-series models (F-test p > 0.10), or 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) acceleration above 2.5% annualized signals robust economic expansion that supports earnings growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current GDPC1 at 16,470.6 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during above-2.5% GDP growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals synchronized supply and labor weakness that portends earnings recession, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month decline > 0.5% AND PAYEMS month-over-month decline > 50,000 persons
evidence_refs: fact:current INDPRO at 103.3759, PAYEMS at 143,774 thousand
regime_hint: synchronized supply and labor contraction or early recession
failure_condition: Joint condition shows no incremental predictive power beyond single-series models (F-test p > 0.10), or SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite dual contraction.
